message:
fix(cancel): use strengthen() for deterministic cancel reason attribution and defuse drop abort on timeout

Three related changes to make cancellation reason tracking deterministic
and prevent spurious abort-on-drop in combinator control flow:

scope.rs (RegionRunner + Select timeout):
  - Change RegionRunner::Output from Fut::Output to
    thread::Result<Fut::Output>, surfacing panics from the inner
    future explicitly rather than unwinding through the runner.
  - Replace unsafe pin projection (get_unchecked_mut + new_unchecked)
    with safe get_mut() + Pin::new(), since the future is already
    Unpin (stored in a Box or directly Unpin).
  - After a Select resolves to the timeout branch (Either::Right),
    call defuse_drop_abort() on the primary JoinFuture. Without this,
    dropping the JoinFuture would fire abort_with_reason(), cancelling
    a task that was merely preempted by timeout—not faulted.

task_handle.rs (abort_with_reason + JoinFuture):
  - abort_with_reason() and JoinFuture::abort_with_reason() now call
    existing.strengthen(&reason) when a cancel reason is already set,
    instead of silently discarding the new reason. This ensures that
    if a parent scope cancels with a weaker reason (e.g., timeout)
    and then a stronger reason arrives (e.g., poll_quota), the
    stronger reason is preserved for diagnostics.
  - Add defuse_drop_abort() method on JoinFuture that sets
    completed=true, preventing the Drop impl from issuing an
    abort when the future is intentionally abandoned (e.g., by
    a Select combinator choosing the other branch).

lab/runtime.rs (poll quota enforcement):
  - Switch from if-is_none / set pattern to if-let-strengthen / else-set
    pattern, matching the task_handle changes and ensuring poll quota
    cancellation reasons compose correctly with any pre-existing reason.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
